title: A constitutional monarchy with parliamentary rule
content: Norway's monarch is head of state, but political authority comes from elected institutions. The core democratic arena is the Storting, which supports and oversees the government.

title: Proportional representation shapes party politics
content: Elections use proportional representation, so multiple parties usually enter parliament. This encourages coalition building and negotiation rather than winner-takes-all government.
next: Coalitions create a specific governing style.

title: Consensus politics over sharp swings
content: Because governments often depend on coalition support, policy change tends to be negotiated and incremental. This reduces sudden reversals and supports long-term planning.
next: Local government carries much of daily implementation.

title: Municipal and county levels matter
content: Municipalities and counties deliver many public services, including schools, planning, and parts of healthcare. National policy is therefore filtered through local administrative capacity.

title: High trust lowers transaction costs
content: Norway's political culture relies on relatively high trust in institutions. High trust makes taxation, regulatory compliance, and social-compromise politics easier to sustain.
next: Still, there are real political tensions.

title: Energy, climate, and regional equity
content: Current politics balances petroleum income against climate goals, and central efficiency against regional inclusion. These tensions test how adaptable the consensus model is.
next: Norway also navigates external alignment choices.

title: EU-outsider, Europe-insider
content: Norway is not an EU member but aligns with much EU regulation through the EEA. Domestic politics therefore includes ongoing debates about sovereignty, integration, and democratic control.
next: The main takeaway is institutional resilience.

title: Norwegian politics is system-heavy, not personality-heavy
content: The country's political stability rests less on single leaders and more on durable rules, party bargaining, local governance, and social trust.
next: Society module: how this system feels in everyday life.
